test.observation: All 19 recency-specific tests passed. Note: Project has 4 pre-existing unrelated test failures in chat-history-pagination.test.ts, db-env-guard.test.ts, and esco.test.ts.

typecheck.observation: Pre-existing TypeScript errors unrelated to this feature (mostly libSQL execute/existing type issues). No new errors introduced by scoring.ts changes.

lint.observation: Pre-existing Biome error in packages/esco/src/esco.ts (unused import). No linting issues introduced by scoring.ts or related files.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add clarification about candidates.lastMatchedAt field usage - ensure workers reference existing schema fields correctly and verify against src/db/schema.ts before implementing features that depend on candidate timestamps.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Recency-aware-scoring feature used candidate.lastMatchedAt field which exists in schema but was not previously documented as available for scoring logic. Reviewer flagged this as knowledge gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When creating library documentation for environment variables, ensure the content is generalizable across missions. Mission-scoped docs should go in mission-specific directories instead.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature recency-aware-scoring created .factory/library/environment.md that only contains recency/quality-specific env var documentation, making it not reusable for other missions.

rejectedObservations.observation: RECENCY_PENALTY_DAYS variable discrepancy noted in code review
rejectedObservations.reason: Implementation detail - both RECENCY_BOOST_DAYS and RECENCY_PENALTY_DAYS exist in .env.example and are properly used. Original task description mentioned RECENTNESS_DECAY_RATE but implementation achieves same goal more explicitly with separate boost/penalty amounts.
